subject: Fwd: Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions
body:
The reference in the attached e-mail to FTI is intended to mean FTC.   Shall 
I tell him that FTC will not agree to be a plaintiff?
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------  
IRS Circular 230 Legend: Any advice contained herein was not intended or  
written to be used, and cannot be used, for the purpose of avoiding U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax penalties. Unless otherwise specifically indicated  
above, you should assume that any statement in this email relating to any U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion 
 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.  
================================================================= 
Confidentiality  Notice: This communication and any attachments contain 
information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or 
that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by 
the intended  recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person 
who receives  this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, 
distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received this communication in error, 
please immediately  delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at 
the above telephone  number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this 
communication is intended to  operate as an electronic signature under applicable  
law.
=================================================================



************************************** See what's new at http://www.aol.com


From: "McCarroll, James C." <JMcCarroll@ReedSmith.com>
Subject: Re: Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions
Date: Mon, 17 Sep 2007 19:02:44 GMT
To: panos@famnyc.com, apacheco@famnyc.com, CMeyeringSciensam@scienscapital.com, dkiesq@aol.com
Cc: "Karides, Constantine" <CKarides@ReedSmith.com>, "Gotthoffer, Lance" <LGotthoffer@ReedSmith.com>

No response yet from Bear.  However, we continue to analyze the prospects for FTI appearing as the plaintiff in a Delaware Chancery Court action to compel Bear to hold the meeting in a timely manner.  We are now cautiously optimistic, subjct to completion of some final analysis by our Delaware attorneys, that this course of action is legally viable, if FTI is willing to appear as the plaintiff.  In case FTI will not ultimately agree to appear as a plaintiff, we also continue to actively seek additional clients who may be able and willing to appear as plaintiffs in a Delaware action.

I am scheduled to speak with senior executives from FTI about this prospect early tomorrow morning.  We will keep you posted.

-Jim



----- Original Message -----
From: Panos Katsambas <panos@famnyc.com>
To: McCarroll, James C.; Abel Pacheco <apacheco@famnyc.com>; CMeyering@Sciensam <CMeyeringSciensam@scienscapital.com>; dkiesq@aol.com <dkiesq@aol.com>
Cc: Karides, Constantine; Gotthoffer, Lance
Sent: Mon Sep 17 12:04:41 2007
Subject: RE: Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions

Any answer to this?

 

________________________________

Panos Katsambas

Fix Asset Management Services, Inc.

660 Madison Avenue, 15th Floor

New York, NY 10021

Tel: +1-212-332-4085

Cell: +1-917-392-4307

Fax: +1-212-758-7660

E-mail: panos@famnyc.com

________________________________

From: McCarroll, James C. [mailto:JMcCarroll@ReedSmith.com] 
Sent: Thursday, September 13, 2007 3:32 PM
To: Panos Katsambas; Abel Pacheco; CMeyering@Sciensam; dkiesq@aol.com
Cc: Karides, Constantine; Gotthoffer, Lance
Subject: FW: Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions

 

Gentlemen-

 

See below and attached.  We will let you know as soon as we receive confirmation that a meeting is being set, or any other response.  In the interim, we continue to analyze potential avenues to accelerate the process.  

 

Please let us know if you have any questions.

 

-Jim

James C. McCarroll 
jmccarroll@reedsmith.com 
Reed Smith LLP
Direct Tel.:  212-549-0209 
Main Line:  212-521-5400 
Facsimile:  212-521-5450 
599 Lexington Avenue
New York, New York  10022
View Bio <http://www.reedsmith.com/our_people.cfm?cit_id=2254&widCall1=customWidgets.content_view_1&usecache=false>  

 

 

________________________________

From: anthony.latuja@americas.bnpparibas.com [mailto:anthony.latuja@americas.bnpparibas.com] 
Sent: Thursday, September 13, 2007 3:07 PM
To: McCarroll, James C.
Cc: renee.fischer@americas.bnpparibas.com; luke.farber@americas.bnpparibas.com; robert.elliott@americas.bnpparibas.com; thomas.prunty@americas.bnpparibas.com
Subject: Re: Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions


Jim, 
 The original executed documents were sent via overnight courier to Walkers Cayman today. I will let you know who signed for the package tomorrow. A copy is attached for your records. 
Thanks, 
Anthony 





Renee FISCHER 

Director
Legal Department
Equities & Derivatives
787 Seventh Avenue, 3rd Floor, 
New York, New York 10019 
tel : + 1 (212) 841-2239   mobile + (917) 207-4482
fax : + 1 (212) 471-8215 

09/12/2007 10:54 AM 

    To

    Anthony LATUJA 

    
cc

    JMcCarroll, Thomas PRUNTY, Luke FARBER, Robert ELLIOTT 

    
Subject

    Fw: Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions

    

 

 

     

    



Anthony, 

Please execute and submit the revised petitions.  A form cover letter is attached as well.  Copy in Jim McCarroll at Reed Smith as well. 

[attachment "HGEL -- Draft BNP Letter re Overseas Petitions.doc" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] 

[attachment "HGEL OVERSEAS -- FTI Petition I.pdf" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] [attachment "HGEL OVERSEAS -- FTI Petition II.pdf" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] [attachment "HGEL OVERSEAS -- FTI Petition III.pdf" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] [attachment "HGEL OVERSEAS -- FTI Petition IV.pdf" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] [attachment "HGEL OVERSEAS -- Schwartz Petition.pdf" deleted by Anthony LATUJA/US/AMERICAS/GROUP] 


Many thanks, 
Renee 
----- Forwarded by Renee FISCHER/US/AMERICAS/GROUP on 09/12/2007 10:51 AM ----- 

Internet 
JMcCarroll@ReedSmith.com 

09/11/2007 03:42 PM 

    To

    Renee FISCHER 

    
cc

     

    
Subject

    Bear Stearns -- Revised Overseas Petitions

    

 

 

     

    



Renee-

As we just discussed, I am attaching five revised overseas petitions, in each instance revised to address Bear Stearns' allegations of defects, along with a draft coverletter that we ask you consider transmitting on your letterhead.

As you know, Bear has asserted (we believe without merit, and in bad faith) that the original petitions were deficient in that you addressed them to the wrong entity (which of course is untrue), and that the fund must have no fewer than two directors (which is inconsistent with the terms of the fund's governing documents).  Bear also has informed us that the new May 31 NAV should be used, and that Sandelovsky had been replaced with Quental, and therefore Quental should be the designated party to be replaced, rather than Sandelovsky.  The attached revised petitions address each of these alleged defects, including, among other things, through adding Bart Schwartz as a proposed replacement director, in addition to FTI.  (Note that Bart Schwartz is a pretty well known former senior official in the SDNY U.S. Attorney's Office (under Giuliani), and has served as independent monitor in connection with federal consent decrees and with many major companies, including Hewlett-Packard.)

Please let me know if you have any questions.  Otherwise, please have the five petitions countersigned by the appropriate BNP representative.  Also, if you find the draft coverletter acceptable, please put it on your letterhead, and send it out, along with the five petitions, at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your ongoing assistance with this matter.

-Jim

* * *

This E-mail, along with any attachments, is considered confidential and may well be legally privileged. If you have received it in error, you are on notice of its status. Please notify us immediately by reply e-mail and then delete this message from your system. Please do not copy it or use it for any purposes, or disclose its contents to any other person. Thank you for your cooperation.
* * *
To ensure compliance with Treasury Department regulations, we inform you that, unless otherwise indicated in writing, any U.S. Federal tax advice contained in this communication  (including any attachments) is not intended or written to be used, and cannot be used, for the purpose of (1) avoiding penalties under the Internal Revenue Code or applicable state and local provisions or (2) promoting, marketing or recommending to another party any tax-related matters addressed herein.
Disclaimer Version RS.US.1.01.03
